problem: I'm supposed to sleep but simply cannot.
stakes: I will be sleep deprived during the day.
move: Force yourself to sleep via hard napping.
outcome: You sleep and wake up energised or at least shift your circadian rhythm by a few hours.

problem: It's past 10 PM and you haven't begun winding down.
stakes: Late sleep, poor sleep quality, next day starts in a hole.
move: Phone down. Dim lights. Do chin tucks and hip flexor stretch in bed.
outcome: Body shifts into sleep mode. You fall asleep faster and deeper.

problem: You're scrolling your phone in bed instead of sleeping.
stakes: Blue light suppresses melatonin. You'll lose 1-2 hours of sleep quality.
move: Put the phone face down on your desk. Not the nightstand — the desk.
outcome: Screen stimulus stops. Sleep onset drops to minutes instead of an hour.

problem: Your room is warm and you're tossing around.
stakes: Core body temp needs to drop for deep sleep. You'll get fragmented rest.
move: Switch on the AC or fan. Aim for 18-20°C.
outcome: Body cools. Sleep onset and deep sleep both improve.

problem: Your mind is running and won't shut off.
stakes: Rumination steals hours. Tomorrow's energy is being spent tonight.
move: Write the thoughts down on paper. Brain dump, don't organize. Close the notebook.
outcome: Externalised thoughts lose their grip. Sleep comes easier.

problem: It's midnight and you suddenly feel wired and productive.
stakes: You'll ride the wave till 3 AM and wreck tomorrow.
move: Acknowledge it. Write one note about what to do with this energy tomorrow. Go to bed.
outcome: The energy is captured without burning the candle. Tomorrow you actually use it.

problem: You woke up and went straight to a dark screen.
stakes: Circadian clock stays delayed. Melatonin lingers. Mornings stay painful.
move: Step onto the balcony for 5 minutes. No sunglasses. Let light hit your eyes.
outcome: Cortisol wakes up on schedule. Alertness kicks in naturally.

problem: You woke up and went straight to coffee or your phone.
stakes: You start the day dehydrated. Energy and focus tank by midday.
move: Drink 500ml warm water with a pinch of salt and 1 tbsp ACV.
outcome: Gut primed, hydration restored, caffeine hits harder when you get to it.

problem: You haven't eaten and it's been hours since waking.
stakes: Blood sugar crashes. You'll overeat later or grab junk.
move: Make the default bowl — oats, whey, frozen blueberries, chia, cinnamon, salt.
outcome: 400 cals, 32g protein locked in. Brain has fuel for the morning.

problem: You're about to order delivery because you don't feel like cooking.
stakes: Excess calories, sodium, and regret. Budget takes a hit too.
move: Cook the simplest thing in your kitchen. Eggs and toast counts.
outcome: You ate real food. Money saved. Self-respect intact.

problem: It's past noon and you've barely hit 30g protein.
stakes: Muscle protein synthesis stalls. Recomp goals get undermined.
move: Eat 200g Greek yogurt with a scoop of whey. 50g protein in 2 minutes.
outcome: Protein target back on track. Muscles get what they need.

problem: You've been working for hours and haven't had water.
stakes: Cognitive function drops. Headache and fatigue creep in.
move: Fill a bottle. Drink 500ml right now.
outcome: Fog lifts within 20 minutes. Energy stabilises.

problem: You want something sweet and the craving is loud.
stakes: One sugary snack spirals into a binge. Blood sugar rollercoaster follows.
move: Eat a handful of berries or dark chocolate. Wait 10 minutes.
outcome: Craving passes. Blood sugar stays stable. No binge.

problem: It's past 2 PM and you're reaching for another coffee.
stakes: Caffeine half-life is 5-6 hours. Your sleep tonight is at risk.
move: Switch to water or green tea. Save the caffeine for tomorrow morning.
outcome: Adenosine clears naturally. Sleep onset tonight stays on track.

problem: You've eaten meals today but haven't logged anything.
stakes: Recomp needs precision. Untracked days compound into stalled progress.
move: Open the app. Log everything you remember from today. Rough is fine.
outcome: Awareness restored. You can course-correct dinner to hit targets.

problem: You've eaten mostly protein and carbs with barely any vegetables.
stakes: Gut health suffers. Visceral fat reduction needs 30g+ fiber daily.
move: Add a side of lentils, beans, or a big salad to your next meal.
outcome: Fiber target approached. Gut microbiome gets what it needs.

problem: You're inhaling food at your desk while working.
stakes: Poor digestion. Satiety signals don't register. You'll overeat.
move: Stop. Sit at the table. No screen. Chew slowly for this one meal.
outcome: You feel full on less. Digestion improves. Meal actually satisfies.

problem: You're about to eat and haven't taken your stack.
stakes: Missed doses compound. Consistency is the entire point of supplementation.
move: Take your fat-soluble stack with this meal — D3+K2, CoQ10, astaxanthin, curcumin, omega-3.
outcome: Stack absorbed. One less thing to remember today.

problem: You haven't taken creatine today.
stakes: Saturation takes days to build and days to lose. Gaps cost you.
move: 5g creatine monohydrate in water. Just drink it.
outcome: Creatine stores maintained. Cognitive and muscular benefits continue.

problem: You've been sitting for 30+ minutes and your posture has collapsed.
stakes: Lower back pain, belly pushed out, neck tension compounds daily.
move: Do a 2-minute stack check — tuck tailbone, ribs down, ears over shoulders, chin tuck.
outcome: Spine resets to neutral. Tension drains from neck and lower back.

problem: You're working from bed. Laptop on chest, neck cranked, spine destroyed.
stakes: Neck blood flow drops, hair falls, back pain locks in, energy crashes.
move: Get up. Move to the desk. Sit properly or stand.
outcome: Posture recovers. Blood flows. You think clearer within minutes.

problem: Morning is slipping away and you haven't moved.
stakes: Visceral fat mobilisation window missed. Energy stays flat.
move: Put on shoes. Walk for 20 minutes. No music, no phone, just walk.
outcome: Fasted fat burning triggered. Head clears. Day has momentum.

problem: It's a training day and you're finding reasons to skip.
stakes: Missed sessions break streaks. Recomp stalls without progressive overload.
move: Do just the first exercise. One set. See what happens.
outcome: You almost always finish once you start. Gains stay on track.

problem: You woke up and went straight to your desk without moving.
stakes: Hip flexors tighten further. APT worsens. Back will ache by noon.
move: Do the couch stretch (90s each side) and 10 cat-cows. That's it.
outcome: Hips open. Spine mobilised. Posture starts the day neutral.

problem: Your neck is stiff and painful from screen time.
stakes: It gets worse every hour you ignore it. Headache incoming.
move: 10 chin tucks, 30 seconds each side neck stretch, 10 wall slides.
outcome: Tension releases. Range of motion returns. Headache averted.

problem: You've been sitting for over 2 hours straight.
stakes: Blood pools. Metabolism slows. Brain fog accumulates.
move: Stand up. Walk to the kitchen and back. 30 seconds of movement.
outcome: Circulation restored. Mental clarity gets a micro-boost.

problem: You didn't do your core work today.
stakes: APT correction requires daily activation. Abs stay asleep, pelvis stays tilted.
move: 3 sets of 10 dead bugs. Press lower back into floor. Slow and controlled.
outcome: Abs wake up. Pelvis learns neutral. Posture improvement compounds.

problem: You've been sitting all day and your glutes are completely dormant.
stakes: Inactive glutes mean your lower back compensates. Pain follows.
move: 3 sets of 15 glute bridges with posterior pelvic tilt. Squeeze hard at the top.
outcome: Glutes fire. Lower back load reduces. APT correction advances.

problem: It's a rest day and you haven't moved at all.
stakes: Recovery needs blood flow. Zero movement means slower repair.
move: Go for a 15-minute easy walk. No intensity, just movement.
outcome: Active recovery engaged. Muscles get nutrients. Mood lifts.

problem: Your shoulders are rounding forward again.
stakes: Upper crossed syndrome deepens. Chest tightens, mid-back weakens.
move: 3 sets of 15 band pull-aparts or face pulls. Squeeze shoulder blades.
outcome: Scapular retractors activate. Shoulders pull back naturally.

problem: You finished training but skipped the cool-down.
stakes: Tight muscles lock in. Recovery takes longer. Injury risk rises.
move: Hip flexor stretch 60s each side, chest doorway stretch 60s each side.
outcome: Muscles release. Tomorrow's soreness reduced. Flexibility maintained.

problem: You're looking down at your phone like everyone else.
stakes: Neck load triples at 45 degrees. Cervical spine degrades.
move: Bring the phone up to eye level. Hold it there.
outcome: Neck load drops to neutral. Small change, huge cumulative difference.

problem: You know what to do but can't begin. The task just sits there.
stakes: The delay snowballs. One skipped task becomes a wasted day.
move: Open the file/app and do only the first 2 minutes. Nothing more.
outcome: Momentum kicks in. The task stops feeling like a wall.

problem: You have 30+ browser tabs open and can't focus on any of them.
stakes: Cognitive load maxed. Every tab is an open loop draining attention.
move: Close all tabs except the one task you need to do right now.
outcome: Visual noise gone. Brain can single-thread again.

problem: You opened social media for 'a second' and it's been 30 minutes.
stakes: Dopamine baseline drops. Real tasks feel even harder now.
move: Close the app. Lock your phone face down. Set a 25-minute work timer.
outcome: Attention reclaimed. One focused block restores your sense of agency.

problem: You keep bouncing between 4 tasks and finishing none.
stakes: Context switching costs 15-25 minutes each time. Nothing gets done.
move: Pick the smallest task. Finish it completely. Then move to the next.
outcome: One thing done. Completion dopamine hits. Queue starts clearing.

problem: You're planning how to do the thing instead of doing the thing.
stakes: Planning feels productive but produces nothing. Hours evaporate.
move: Stop planning. Do the ugliest possible first draft in 10 minutes.
outcome: A bad draft exists. Editing a bad draft is easier than creating from nothing.

problem: You have a full to-do list but no idea what matters most.
stakes: You'll do the easy stuff and avoid the important stuff. Day feels busy but empty.
move: Pick the one thing that would make today feel like a win. Do that first.
outcome: Priority clarity. Even if everything else falls apart, the day was useful.

problem: It's 3 PM and your ability to concentrate has vanished.
stakes: Forcing it produces bad work. Ignoring it wastes the afternoon.
move: Switch to a low-cognitive task — organise files, reply to messages, clean your desk.
outcome: Afternoon isn't wasted. You do useful work that doesn't need deep focus.

problem: A shiny new idea just hit and you want to drop everything for it.
stakes: Another false start. The current project stalls. Pattern repeats.
move: Write the idea in a capture note. One sentence. Return to what you were doing.
outcome: Idea is saved. Current work continues. You can revisit later with fresh eyes.

problem: It's well into work hours and you still haven't opened your design tool.
stakes: Anxiety builds the longer you wait. Productivity window shrinks.
move: Open Figma. Look at one frame. That's the only commitment.
outcome: The hardest part was opening it. Flow usually follows.

problem: There's one task you keep pushing to tomorrow.
stakes: Avoidance debt compounds with interest. It only gets harder.
move: Set a 15-minute timer. Work only on that task. Stop when it rings.
outcome: Progress made. The task shrinks. Often you'll keep going past the timer.

problem: You can't decide between options and you're frozen.
stakes: No decision is the worst decision. Time passes, nothing happens.
move: Pick the option you'd explain most easily to someone else. Go with it.
outcome: Movement beats perfection. You can course-correct later.

problem: Work is done and you feel the pull toward doomscrolling, gaming, or lying in bed.
stakes: You lose 5 hours. Wake up feeling worse than before the evening started.
move: Name it out loud: 'I'm reward-seeking right now.' Pick one thing — walk, cook, read — for 30 minutes.
outcome: The spiral breaks. You end the evening with something real instead of regret.

problem: Brain fog hit. You're staring at your screen doing nothing.
stakes: You'll push through poorly or waste the rest of the afternoon.
move: Run the reset — drink water, eat something, cold water face wash, 15-min hard nap.
outcome: Energy rebounds enough to salvage the second half of the day.

problem: You haven't brushed your teeth, showered, or changed clothes today.
stakes: Self-respect erodes quietly. The spiral from here is familiar.
move: Brush teeth right now. That's it. Just teeth.
outcome: One act of care breaks the inertia. Shower and clean clothes usually follow.

problem: It's afternoon and you haven't showered.
stakes: Physical discomfort drags mood down. You feel less like a person.
move: Take a shower right now. Hot or cold, doesn't matter. Just get in.
outcome: State change is instant. You feel human again.

problem: Your eyes are dry, red, or aching from screen time.
stakes: Eye strain compounds into headaches and further focus loss.
move: Close eyes for 60 seconds. Then look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.
outcome: Eye muscles relax. Dryness eases. You bought yourself another hour.

problem: You feel tension rising but can't name why.
stakes: Unmanaged stress becomes cortisol. Cortisol becomes visceral fat, poor sleep, and bad decisions.
move: 10 slow exhales. Longer out than in. Count if it helps.
outcome: Parasympathetic nervous system activates. The edge softens.

problem: Work just ended and you've collapsed on the bed with no transition.
stakes: No boundary between work and rest. Evening bleeds into numbness.
move: Change clothes. Walk to the kitchen. Make tea or snack. This is the transition.
outcome: Work mode exits. Recovery mode enters. Evening becomes intentional.

problem: It's Sunday evening and you did nothing meaningful all weekend.
stakes: Monday starts with guilt. The cycle continues.
move: Do one small thing right now. Clean one surface. Prep one meal. Lay out tomorrow's clothes.
outcome: Weekend ends on a note of agency. Monday is slightly easier.

problem: You broke all your routines today and feel like a failure.
stakes: Shame makes tomorrow worse. One bad day becomes a bad week.
move: Say it: 'One bad day. Not a pattern. Tomorrow's a new run.'
outcome: Self-compassion stops the bleed. Tomorrow you start fresh.

problem: You want food not because you're hungry but because you're feeling something.
stakes: Emotional eating reinforces the cycle. Calories wasted, feeling unchanged.
move: Name the emotion. Say it out loud. Drink water. Wait 10 minutes.
outcome: The urge often passes once the feeling is named. If you still want to eat, at least it's conscious.

problem: You're about to sleep and haven't taken your evening supplements.
stakes: Magnesium glycinate supports sleep quality. Missing it is missing low-hanging fruit.
move: Take your magnesium and omega-3. Two pills, done.
outcome: Sleep quality improves. Muscle recovery overnight gets a boost.

problem: You need to focus today but skipped your morning L-tyrosine.
stakes: Dopamine precursor missed on a day you actually need it.
move: Take 500mg L-tyrosine on empty stomach. Wait 30 min before eating.
outcome: Dopamine substrate available. Focus and motivation get a genuine edge.

problem: Your Figma file is sprawling with 20 explorations visible.
stakes: Stakeholders see chaos. Your best work gets lost in noise.
move: Hide all workspace. Show only 1-3 options, fully simplified, all cases covered.
outcome: File looks professional. Review goes smoother. Your work speaks clearly.

problem: You're about to start designing without asking any questions.
stakes: You'll build the wrong thing. Revisions multiply. Time wasted.
move: Front-load with questions. Ask about the flow, edge cases, and dev constraints first.
outcome: Requirements are clear. You design once instead of three times.

problem: You're about to present work without checking the PM's dashboard.
stakes: You'll be caught off-guard by numbers. Credibility takes a hit.
move: Open the PM's dashboard. Spend 5 minutes understanding what the data says.
outcome: You walk into the meeting informed. Numbers back your design decisions.

problem: A high-stakes meeting is in 30 minutes and you're spiralling.
stakes: Panic produces bad presentations. You say things you don't mean.
move: 10 slow breaths. Open your files. Verify everything is ready. Preparation kills panic.
outcome: You walk in calm and prepared. Confidence comes from readiness.

problem: Stakeholder keeps asking for changes that don't improve the design.
stakes: Your energy drains. Quality drops. Resentment builds.
move: Ask: 'What specific problem does this change solve?' Redirect to the user need.
outcome: Conversation shifts from opinion to evidence. Unnecessary revisions stop.

problem: You're about to show design work but haven't structured your walkthrough.
stakes: Unstructured presentation invites random feedback. Meeting devolves into colour preferences.
move: Lead with the problem (2 min), then show the solution in a user scenario (5 min).
outcome: Audience evaluates work against the problem, not personal taste.

problem: Clutter everywhere. Surfaces covered. It's weighing on you.
stakes: Messy environment feeds messy thinking. The longer you wait the worse it gets.
move: Set a 15-minute timer. Clean one room only. Surfaces first, then floor.
outcome: One clean room changes how the whole house feels. Momentum follows.

problem: Old food is sitting in the fridge and you keep ignoring it.
stakes: Stale food makes cooking feel gross. You order out instead.
move: Take everything out. Toss expired items. Wipe shelves. Put back what's fresh.
outcome: Fridge is clean. Cooking becomes possible again. Money saved.

problem: Dirty clothes are accumulating and you're running out of clean ones.
stakes: You start wearing bad clothes. Self-image drops. Spiral gets closer.
move: Start a load right now. You don't need to fold today — just wash.
outcome: Clean clothes exist again. One less thing degrading your day.

problem: It's Sunday and you haven't done your weekly clean.
stakes: Monday starts in a dirty house. Mood tanks before work even begins.
move: Follow the protocol: garbage → laundry → kitchen → bathroom → living room → floors.
outcome: House is reset. Week starts clean. Mental clarity follows physical order.

problem: Your workspace is buried under random stuff.
stakes: Visual clutter fragments attention. Every object is an open loop.
move: Clear the desk. Only keyboard, mouse, monitor, and water bottle remain.
outcome: Clean workspace. Focus improves immediately. Professional environment restored.

problem: It's been days since you had a real conversation with another person.
stakes: Isolation deepens silently. Social muscles atrophy. Loneliness compounds.
move: Send one message to one friend. Doesn't need to be deep. Just reach out.
outcome: Connection maintained. The isolation pattern gets interrupted.

problem: You need to call someone — broker, dentist, friend — and keep putting it off.
stakes: Avoidance breeds anxiety. The call takes 5 minutes, the dread takes days.
move: Dial right now. No script needed. Just say what you need.
outcome: Done. Relief floods in. One less thing on the avoidance pile.

problem: You're being vague about your intentions with someone.
stakes: Unclear communication hurts both of you. Trust erodes.
move: Be direct. Say what you actually want or don't want. One clear sentence.
outcome: Respect earned. Relationship clarified. Guilt released.

problem: You were invited somewhere and you're thinking of skipping.
stakes: Every skip makes the next one easier. Network atrophies.
move: Go for 30 minutes. Set a hard exit time. Leave when it's up if you want.
outcome: You showed up. That's 90% of it. Sometimes you even enjoy it.

problem: You need to communicate something to a colleague but keep dancing around it.
stakes: Ambiguity creates rework. People don't know where you stand.
move: Write it out first. Then say it simply. Lead with the conclusion.
outcome: Message lands. No confusion. You save both of you time.

problem: Your social life is on life support and you've done nothing about it.
stakes: Friendships die from neglect, not conflict. The window closes quietly.
move: Pick 3 people. Send each a short message today. Not a catch-up essay — just a ping.
outcome: Three threads reopened. Some will reply. Connection restored.

problem: You saw someone's achievements online and feel like shit.
stakes: Comparison steals the rest of your day. Shame triggers old coping patterns.
move: Close the app. Write down one thing you did this week that you're genuinely proud of.
outcome: Perspective returns. Their highlight reel isn't your scorecard.

problem: You're about to buy something you don't need.
stakes: Small impulse purchases compound. Budget bleeds silently.
move: Add it to a wishlist. Wait 48 hours. If you still want it, buy it.
outcome: Most impulses die in 48 hours. Money stays where it belongs.

problem: You have no idea what you've spent this month.
stakes: Financial blindness leads to overspending and anxiety.
move: Open your bank app. Review the last 7 days of transactions. That's enough.
outcome: Awareness is 80% of the fix. You make better choices this week.

problem: You haven't moved money to savings yet this month.
stakes: Emergency fund stays weak. Future self pays for present neglect.
move: Transfer a fixed amount to savings right now. Even ₹5000 counts.
outcome: Savings habit maintained. The number grows quietly.

problem: You set a 1-hour gaming limit and you're about to blow past it.
stakes: Every extra hour steals from sleep, goals, and self-trust.
move: Finish the current match. Close the game. Walk away from the desk.
outcome: Limit respected. Self-discipline muscle gets stronger. Sleep protected.

problem: The urge to watch porn just hit.
stakes: Dopamine crash follows. ED worsens. Shame spiral activates.
move: Leave the room. Do 20 pushups or take a cold face wash. Break the physical state.
outcome: Urge passes in 10 minutes if you change your physiology. Streak continues.

problem: You haven't decided what to wear tomorrow and mornings are chaos.
stakes: Decision fatigue hits before the day even starts.
move: Lay out clothes tonight. Takes 2 minutes. One less decision tomorrow.
outcome: Morning flows smoother. Cognitive budget preserved for real work.

problem: Work just stopped but there was no clean ending.
stakes: Without closure, work thoughts bleed into evening. Recovery suffers.
move: Write tomorrow's top 3 tasks. Close all work apps. Say 'done' out loud.
outcome: Brain gets permission to stop. Evening becomes yours.

problem: First thing you did was check notifications.
stakes: Other people's priorities hijack your morning before you set your own.
move: Put the phone in another room until after breakfast and morning water.
outcome: First hour is yours. Priorities get set by you, not your inbox.

problem: You captured information this week but haven't processed it into notes.
stakes: Unprocessed info rots. Your knowledge system becomes a graveyard.
move: Spend 15 minutes processing one note. Turn it into a practice, protocol, or principle.
outcome: One piece of knowledge becomes actionable. System stays alive.

problem: You're beating yourself up internally.
stakes: Self-criticism doesn't motivate — it paralyses. Performance drops.
move: Catch the thought. Ask: 'Would I say this to someone I respect?' Reframe it.
outcome: Inner critic quiets. You can act from clarity instead of shame.

problem: Work stress hit and you've retreated to bed with your laptop.
stakes: This is the pattern. Bed → bad posture → forget to eat → hours lost → guilt.
move: Sit up. Feet on floor. Take 3 breaths. Then move to the desk.
outcome: You interrupted the cascade at step one. Everything downstream improves.

problem: It's mid-afternoon and you've had nothing but coffee.
stakes: Blood sugar tanked hours ago. You're running on cortisol and willpower.
move: Eat anything. Eggs, fruit, a protein bar. Don't optimise, just eat.
outcome: Blood sugar stabilises. Brain comes back online. Crisis averted.

problem: Fridge is empty and you've been ordering out for days.
stakes: No ingredients means no cooking. Junk food becomes the default.
move: Order groceries online right now. Stick to the staples list: oats, eggs, chicken, veggies, berries.
outcome: Groceries arrive. Cooking becomes possible. Default meals return.

problem: Your wardrobe is a mess and getting dressed feels stressful.
stakes: Chaos breeds chaos. You wear whatever's on top and feel worse for it.
move: Spend 15 minutes folding and organising. Group by type.
outcome: Getting dressed becomes a 1-minute decision. Self-presentation improves.

problem: You did the workout but used the same weights as last time.
stakes: No overload means no adaptation. You're maintaining, not growing.
move: Add one rep or 1kg to at least one exercise today.
outcome: Progressive overload applied. Muscle growth signal sent.

problem: It's a training day and you're eating like a rest day.
stakes: Calorie cycling only works if you actually cycle. Muscles need fuel on training days.
move: Add a serving of rice or extra oats to your next meal. Hit 280g carbs today.
outcome: Training day fuelled properly. Glycogen full. Performance up.

problem: It's a rest day and you're eating like it's training day.
stakes: Surplus on rest days stores as fat. Recomp math breaks.
move: Check your macros. Drop carbs to 155g today. Keep protein at 170g.
outcome: Rest day deficit pulls from fat. Recomp stays on track.

problem: You have nothing pre-made and every meal requires a decision.
stakes: Decision fatigue at meal time leads to junk food or skipping meals entirely.
move: Premix your dry oats jar: oats, chia, flax, cinnamon, salt in one container.
outcome: Tomorrow's breakfast is a scoop and cook. Zero decisions required.

problem: You fell off all routines and it feels impossible to start again.
stakes: The longer you wait to restart, the harder it gets. Inertia wins.
move: Pick the easiest routine you have. Do just that one today. Nothing else.
outcome: One routine done. The 'I can't' story breaks. Tomorrow add one more.

problem: You've been consuming content for hours without doing anything with it.
stakes: Input without output is entertainment disguised as productivity.
move: Stop consuming. Open a note. Write one actionable takeaway from what you just read.
outcome: Information becomes knowledge. The practice/protocol/principle framework activates.

problem: You're training but not logging sets, reps, or weights.
stakes: Without tracking, progressive overload is guesswork. Gains stall.
move: Log today's workout. Even a quick note on your phone counts.
outcome: Next session you know exactly what to beat. Progress becomes visible.

problem: You're drowsy and sluggish but a nap isn't an option.
stakes: Sluggishness becomes the default for the rest of the day.
move: Splash cold water on your face 5-6 times. Jaw, cheeks, forehead.
outcome: Mammalian dive reflex activates. Alertness spikes immediately.

problem: You haven't been outside at all today.
stakes: No sunlight, no movement, no change of environment. Cabin fever sets in.
move: Walk around the block once. 10 minutes. That's all.
outcome: Sunlight, fresh air, and a change of scenery. Mood lifts. Perspective shifts.

problem: You've been sitting so long your hip flexors are screaming.
stakes: Tight hip flexors pull pelvis forward. APT gets worse with every hour.
move: Couch stretch, 90 seconds each side. Use the wall or your bed.
outcome: Hip flexors release. Pelvis tilts back toward neutral. Relief is immediate.

problem: It's past work hours and you're checking Slack or email.
stakes: No boundary means no recovery. Burnout accelerates silently.
move: Close the work app. Whatever it is can wait until tomorrow.
outcome: Evening is recovered. Mental energy starts replenishing.

problem: You need to book a dental appointment and keep avoiding it.
stakes: Teeth don't fix themselves. Delay means bigger problems and bigger bills.
move: Call and book. Right now. It takes 2 minutes.
outcome: Appointment made. Dread gone. Problem on a timeline to be solved.

problem: It's evening and you've barely walked 2000 steps.
stakes: Sedentary days accumulate into metabolic slowdown and stiffness.
move: Take a 20-minute after-dinner walk. Barefoot in the park if possible.
outcome: Steps closer to 8000. Digestion improves. Mind unwinds before bed.
